description: A plain candid documentary photograph with no designed on-image text or agency graphics (the OCR 'text' is garbled noise from the photo). The scene is inside an automotive/vocational shop classroom: several young men, apparently students, stand in a row wearing clear safety goggles, smiling and looking off-camera as if listening to or greeting someone. The central young man wears a black t-shirt reading 'TEXAS ... WRESTLING STATE CHAMPIONS'. A red vehicle is raised on a lift in the background along with shop equipment, an engine block, and floor jacks. In the foreground (out of focus) is the back/shoulder of an adult in a plaid shirt, suggesting a visiting official touring the class. Naturalistic photography, real image, not a poster or meme. Used by USDOL to illustrate skilled-trades / apprenticeship education in support of its 'American Workers First' messaging.
notable_text: TEXAS WRESTLING STATE CHAMPIONS (on student's t-shirt)
